title: Dashboard page with stage cards
analysis:
This task builds the primary pipeline dashboard page. The existing codebase has: (1) FastAPI app factory in app.py that registers routers and configures Jinja2 templates; (2) CatalogDB with full CRUD for pipeline_runs (get_current_run, list_runs, insert_run, update_run), pipeline_jobs (list_jobs, count_jobs_by_status, insert_job, update_job), pipeline_gates (get_all_gates, get_gate, update_gate, init_default_gates), and pipeline_events (insert_event, get_events_since, prune_events); (3) SSE endpoint at /api/events streaming pipeline events via sse-starlette; (4) base.html template with Tailwind CSS (CDN), HTMX, dark theme, and nav (already has /dashboard link); (5) app.js with SSE connectSSE() stub and toast system; (6) app.css with toast styles.

The 9 pipeline stages are defined in CatalogDB._PIPELINE_STAGES: ingest, analyze, classify, narrate, script, edl, source, render, upload.

Key DB schema: pipeline_runs has run_id, started_at, finished_at, config_snapshot, current_stage, status, wall_clock_seconds, budget_remaining_seconds. pipeline_jobs has job_id, stage, job_type, target_id, target_label, status, started_at, finished_at, duration_seconds, progress_pct, error_message, worker, run_id. pipeline_gates has stage, mode, status, decided_at, decided_by, notes, timeout_hours.

Approach: Create dashboard.py route module with GET /dashboard (HTML page), GET / (redirect to /dashboard), GET /api/run (JSON current run status), GET /api/stages (JSON stage summaries). Create dashboard.html template extending base.html with a responsive grid of 9 stage cards, pipeline timeline bar, and 'no active run' banner. Create stage_card.html partial for HTMX OOB swaps. Create timeline_bar.html partial. Wire SSE events in app.js to trigger HTMX fetches for stage card partials. Register dashboard_router in routes/__init__.py and app.py.

The test pattern follows test_sse.py and test_media_detail.py: file-backed CatalogDB fixture, create_app(db_path), TestClient. Seed with pipeline runs, jobs across stages, gates, and events to validate rendering.

prerequisites.description: Create tests/test_dashboard.py with fixtures: file-backed CatalogDB seeded with a running pipeline run (run_id='run-1', started_at, current_stage='analyze', budget_remaining_seconds=3600), jobs across multiple stages (e.g. 5 done + 2 running in ingest, 1 running + 2 pending in analyze, 1 error in classify), initialized default gates with some modified (analyze=manual), and a few error events. Fixtures: dashboard_db_path, dashboard_db, dashboard_seeded_db, dashboard_app, dashboard_client. Follows patterns from test_sse.py and test_media_detail.py.

steps.description: Write failing tests for GET /dashboard: (1) returns 200 with text/html; (2) contains all 9 stage names (ingest, analyze, classify, narrate, script, edl, source, render, upload); (3) extends base.html (contains 'Autopilot Video' nav); (4) contains 'Dashboard' in page title; (5) when no active run exists, shows 'Pipeline not running' banner.

Create autopilot/web/routes/dashboard.py with GET /dashboard route and _get_db helper. Create dashboard.html template extending base.html with 9 stage cards in a responsive Tailwind grid (lg:grid-cols-3, md:grid-cols-2, grid-cols-1). Create partials/stage_card.html with stage name, status placeholder, and id='stage-{name}' for HTMX targeting. Add 'Pipeline not running' conditional banner. Register dashboard_router in routes/__init__.py and include in app.py.

Write failing tests for stage card content with seeded data: (1) ingest card shows green/done status (all 5 jobs done); (2) analyze card shows blue/running status (has running jobs); (3) classify card shows red/error status (has error job); (4) cards show progress text like '5/5' for ingest, '0/3' for analyze; (5) gate badges show correct mode ('auto' for ingest, 'manual' for analyze).

Implement dashboard route logic: query get_current_run(), count_jobs_by_status() for each stage filtered by run_id, get_all_gates(). Compute per-stage: aggregate status (idle/running/waiting/done/error), done/total counts, gate mode. Add status-to-color mapping (grey=idle, blue=running, amber=waiting, green=done, red=error) using Tailwind classes in stage_card.html. Render progress bar and gate badge in each card.

Write failing tests for pipeline timeline bar: (1) dashboard HTML contains overall progress percentage element; (2) contains budget consumed vs remaining display (wall_clock_seconds and budget_remaining_seconds from run); (3) contains run duration display; (4) timeline bar not rendered when no active run.

Create partials/timeline_bar.html with: overall progress bar (total done jobs / total jobs across all stages), budget display (wall_clock_seconds formatted as elapsed, budget_remaining_seconds formatted as remaining), run start time, estimated completion. Wire into dashboard.html below stage cards grid with id='timeline-bar'.

Write failing tests for JSON API endpoints: (1) GET /api/run returns 200 with JSON containing 'run' key; (2) when active run exists, 'run' has run_id, status, current_stage, wall_clock_seconds fields; (3) when no active run, 'run' is null; (4) GET /api/stages returns 200 with JSON list of 9 stage objects; (5) each stage object has 'name', 'status_counts' (dict with pending/running/done/error counts), 'gate_mode' fields.

Implement GET /api/run and GET /api/stages JSON endpoints in dashboard.py. /api/run queries get_current_run() and returns {'run': run_dict_or_null}. /api/stages iterates _PIPELINE_STAGES, calls count_jobs_by_status(stage, run_id) and get_gate(stage), returns list of stage summary dicts.

Write failing tests for HTMX stage card partial endpoint: (1) GET /dashboard/stage/ingest returns 200 with HTML containing stage name and status; (2) response includes hx-swap-oob or id='stage-ingest' for targeted swap; (3) returns 404 for invalid stage name; (4) card partial reflects current DB state (e.g. after updating a job to done, re-fetching shows updated progress).

Implement GET /dashboard/stage/{stage_name} endpoint that validates stage against _PIPELINE_STAGES, queries current run and job counts for that stage, renders stage_card.html partial with hx-swap-oob='true' and id='stage-{name}'. Return 404 for invalid stage names.

Write tests verifying SSE integration in app.js: (1) app.js contains EventSource or connectSSE usage for '/api/events'; (2) app.js has event handlers for 'stage_started', 'stage_completed', and 'job_progress' event types; (3) handlers contain htmx.ajax or fetch call to '/dashboard/stage/' for card updates.

Update app.js: on DOMContentLoaded, if page contains '#dashboard-grid' element, call connectSSE('/api/events') and add event listeners for stage_started, stage_completed, job_progress. Each handler extracts the stage name from event data and calls htmx.ajax('GET', '/dashboard/stage/{stage}', {target: '#stage-{stage}', swap: 'outerHTML'}) to update the specific card. Update app.css with stage card status color utility classes (bg-stage-idle, bg-stage-running, etc.) and progress bar animation styles.
Write failing test for GET / redirect: verify GET / returns redirect (307 or 302) to /dashboard.
Add GET / route to dashboard.py that uses RedirectResponse to redirect to /dashboard. Verify all tests pass end-to-end.

design_decisions.decision: Dashboard route at /dashboard (not /), with / redirecting to /dashboard
design_decisions.rationale: base.html nav already links to /dashboard. Having a dedicated path keeps URL semantics clean while / provides a convenient entry point. Redirect is 307 to preserve HTTP method semantics.

design_decisions.decision: Query job counts per stage using existing count_jobs_by_status() CatalogDB method
design_decisions.rationale: CatalogDB already has count_jobs_by_status(stage, run_id) returning {status: count} dicts. No new DB methods needed -- call it for each of the 9 stages filtered by current run_id. For 9 stages this is 9 simple indexed queries, which is fast enough.

design_decisions.decision: HTMX partial endpoint at GET /dashboard/stage/{stage_name} for SSE-driven updates
design_decisions.rationale: SSE events from /api/events trigger JS to fetch individual card partials from the server. This avoids duplicating data serialization in JavaScript -- the server renders HTML directly. Matches the existing HTMX partial pattern in media.py tab endpoints.

design_decisions.decision: Stage status derived from job counts rather than a separate status field
design_decisions.rationale: Status logic: error = any job has status='error'; running = any job has status='running'; done = all jobs status='done' and total > 0; waiting = gate mode != 'auto' and status='idle' and no running/error jobs; idle = no jobs for this stage in current run. This avoids needing additional status tracking beyond what already exists.

design_decisions.decision: Use responsive CSS grid (not horizontal scroll) for stage cards
design_decisions.rationale: A responsive grid (3 cols on large, 2 on medium, 1 on small) is more usable than horizontal scroll which hides cards. Tailwind utility classes make this trivial: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3.

design_decisions.decision: Timeline bar as a separate Jinja2 partial template
design_decisions.rationale: Allows independent HTMX updates of the timeline bar without re-rendering the entire page. Can be refreshed on run_progress SSE events via a separate partial endpoint.
media.py already has _format_duration(seconds) for HH:MM:SS formatting. Dashboard needs the same for elapsed time and ETA. Will either import or duplicate this small utility.

reuse.how: Use get_current_run(), count_jobs_by_status(stage, run_id), get_all_gates(), get_gate(stage), list_jobs(stage=..., status='error', run_id=...) for error counting. No new DB methods needed.
Reference for the canonical list of 9 stages to iterate when building dashboard data. Use as validation for stage_name in partial endpoint.

reuse.what: Test fixture pattern from test_sse.py and test_media_detail.py
reuse.how: Use file-backed CatalogDB fixture (tmp_path / 'catalog.db'), create_app(db_path), TestClient pattern. Seed with pipeline_runs, pipeline_jobs, pipeline_gates data using existing insert_run, insert_job, init_default_gates, update_gate methods.
Follow existing pattern: create APIRouter in dashboard.py, export as dashboard_router from routes/__init__.py, app.include_router(dashboard_router) in create_app().
Extend base.html using {% extends 'base.html' %}, override title/content/scripts blocks. Use Tailwind utility classes consistent with existing templates (dark theme, surface colors, gray text).
Extend with dashboard-specific event listeners for stage_started, stage_completed, job_progress events. connectSSE already returns the EventSource object.
Reuse the seconds-to-HH:MM:SS formatting for elapsed time and ETA display. Either import from media module or duplicate the small helper.
Use templates.get_template('partials/stage_card.html').render(...) for partial responses returned as HTMLResponse, matching the existing tab endpoint pattern.
